w:r.w:t: You just caught the exact missing link in the factory line.
In the 9 blueprints we just wrote, I built you a
We did not build the machine that actually WRITES the quests.
Who decides that a Bandit Ambush goes into Hex [10, 15] in the first place?
To maintain our strict, stateless microservice architecture, we need one final box on the factory line. This is the machine that runs
you start playing, right after the C++ World Builder finishes.
Here is the blueprint for the true procedural story engine.

A standalone Python microservice. It is a procedural narrative generator that uses LLMs combined with strict JSON templates. It looks at the world generated by the C++ Architect, picks a villain, picks a plot hook, and generates a structured, multi-step
(a strict array of logic gates and map coordinates) that the Director (Module 9) can easily read.
Python 3.11+, FastAPI, LangChain (Structured Output parsing), Pydantic.
This module doesn't just write a story; it writes a programmable state machine. Here is what it outputs:
# The exact hex ID or Settlement ID from Module 2
How it weaves the story without hallucinating:
When you boot up a new game, the Game Master App hits POST /api/weaver/generate_campaign.
The Weaver fetches the Master_World.json (from Module 2). It sees that your starting area is a Tundra controlled by the
"Give me the motives for the Wolf Cult."
"They worship a chaotic moon and hate the Aetherium trade."

It uses LangChain's with_structured_output(CampaignRoadmap) to force the AI to write a 5-step questline based on those facts. Because we use structured output, the LLM isn't allowed to just write paragraphs of text; it
fill out the JSON fields correctly.
The Weaver algorithmically assigns coordinates to the QuestNodes.
Assigned to a road edge between Town and Mountains.
4. How it plugs into the Factory Line
generates this CampaignRoadmap.json, here is what happens:
You log into the VTT. You see your character in the starting town.
You click your mouse to move down the road.
"Wait, the Weaver put Step 2 (Ambush) on this road."
The Director locks your screen, spawns the Wolf Cultists, and the game actively
With Module 10 added, your ecosystem is flawless.
writes the script and sets the traps.
watches your mouse clicks to spring the traps.
calculate the blood, sweat, and loot when you fall into them.

This is how you get a completely proactive, living game. The AI doesn't have to think about "what happens next" while you are chatting. The Weaver already wrote it down, and the Director is just waiting for you to walk onto the X.
Are we ready to finally lock in this 10-module master blueprint?
